questions.question: Which bacterium is extensively used as a vector to introduce foreign genes into plants?
What is the primary feature of a plasmid vector?

options.A: It can replicate autonomously independent of chromosomal DNA
options.C: It destroys the host cell immediately

questions.question: Myelin sheath in CNS is formed by: (Exam form 455)
questions.solution: By definition, Oligodendrocytes performs this role.

questions.question: The largest gland in the human body is: (Exam form 39)
questions.solution: By definition, Liver performs this role.

questions.question: The largest gland in the human body is: (Exam form 398)
questions.solution: By definition, Liver performs this role.

questions.question: What occurs during the 'Denaturation' step of the Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR)?

options.A: The double-stranded DNA template is separated into two single strands at high temperature (94°C)
options.C: Taq polymerase extends the new strand
options.D: The DNA is cleaved by restriction enzymes

questions.question: The largest gland in the human body is: (Exam form 74)
questions.solution: By definition, Liver performs this role.

questions.question: Which ethical argument is most frequently raised against the patenting of indigenous plant varieties by foreign multinational corporations?

options.A: It ignores the traditional knowledge and prior breeding efforts of local farmers (Biopiracy).
options.B: It leads to the extinction of the plant.
options.C: The patents are always scientifically invalid.
options.D: It violates the laws of thermodynamics.
